behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: System. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because the failure boundary includes real git graph/age evaluation via subprocess + filesystem state (real infra), and mocks can miss ancestry/time edge behavior. Evidence: behavior requires "commits not in main" and "older than the configured age threshold" together with PR absence, which is where real repo state interactions fail. No LLM dependence, no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, make main/feature histories with controlled commit dates, run the production staleness detector through its normal git subprocess path, and verify stale is true only when branch has non-main commits older than threshold and no PR. Use real filesystem + git CLI; PR lookup can be a real HTTP test server if the code path is HTTP-based.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test uses real subprocess/filesystem boundaries that define the failure.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: System. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because this behavior is about session bootstrap signaling, which can fail at real startup wiring/timing and current-branch resolution paths that only appear in real process/session execution. Evidence: "Session-start setup emits" and "current branch" indicate runtime bootstrap + repo context, not just pure function logic. No LLM output quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the real session-start entrypoint in a temp repo on a stale branch, capture emitted startup warnings, and assert the warning appears once with actionable branch context (branch name, stale reason, next action). Exercise actual bootstrap lifecycle and git branch discovery.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Description requires real startup/runtime boundary, matching System.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: Unit. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration with quoted evidence: behavior explicitly says "A local status-classifier function" and "deterministically," so failure is within one pure mapping boundary, no I/O/module handoff required. Higher levels are disqualified by the behavior text itself.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven test of the classifier function with representative computed branch-state inputs, asserting exact label outputs (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) and stable deterministic behavior across repeated runs.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Single-function deterministic mapping; lower-level test fully matches failure boundary.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: System. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration by direct evidence: behavior says "against live repo and PR state," which requires real subprocess/network-backed state; mocks can always pass while hiding real API/repo mismatches. No model-output dependence, so not Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Run `/kaizen-wip` end-to-end in a real repo context with real PR state source, then assert rendered output switches between clean and STALE for controlled branch/PR scenarios. Validate actual rendering output from the command path, not internal helpers.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Live repo + PR dependency requires System-level infrastructure.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: System. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because false positives often arise at real PR-state boundary semantics (open vs merged) when combined with repo state; stubs can hide status-shape and timing mismatches. Evidence: behavior hinges on real PR lifecycle states: "open or merged PRs are not flagged." No LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise detector/status path with real repo branches tied to PRs in open and merged states, and assert classification/output never returns `stale-no-pr` for those branches. Use production PR-state retrieval path and real git state.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: PR lifecycle semantics are validated at real integration boundary.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Top choice: System. Runner-up: Integration. Rejected Integration because rebase/squash correctness depends on real git history transformations and ancestry resolution under changed hashes; synthetic mocks miss these graph semantics. Evidence: behavior explicitly cites "rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change." No LLM or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp real git repo, create feature work, then perform rebase and squash-merge variants that rewrite hashes; run staleness detection via production path and assert branch is no longer classified as unPRed stale when changes are effectively integrated.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real git rewrite operations are necessary to expose this failure mode.
